title: WUOT GM departure

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Tennessee Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to the Tennessee Public Records Act, I request copies of the following records concerning the departure of Jody Hamblett from WUOT at the University of Tennessee:

1. Any resignation letter or written notice of separation submitted by Hamblett.
2. Any written acceptance or acknowledgment of her resignation or separation.
3. Any record reflecting her final date of employment.
4. Any separation agreement, settlement agreement, release, or similar document.

Please provide records in electronic form if possible. If any portion of these records is exempt, please provide the nonexempt portions with redactions and cite the specific legal basis for each withholding. Please notify me in advance if fees will exceed $25.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 7 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Kent Hoover, a citizen of Tennessee, in coordination with Mike Janssen.

title: CPRA - Ventura County - Flock Safety Documents (Ventura County Sheriff's Department)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

- Organization Audit in Flock. 
All data logged from July 1, 2023 to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, this report is available within the Insights tab and reflects searches conducted within the agency.

- Network Audit in Flock. 
All data logged from July 1, 2023 to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, this report is available within the Insights tab and reflects searches of the agency's Flock network by any agency in the Flock system.

- Hardware.
The current number and installed locations of any Flock Safety, LPR,  AVIS, or machine learning-assisted camera systems (provide locations as coordinates or address with nearest cross-street), and any documents related to planned future installations of the same.

- Documents.
>  Invoices, contracts, purchase orders, and bid documents related to the acquisition or renewal of Flock Safety products or services
>  Agreements with homeowner associations, private businesses, or individuals for access to Flock Safety systems
>  Training materials, policies, and SOPs related to Flock Safety platform use
>  Grant or funding records related to the Flock system
>  Data retention policies for data collected by Flock Safety systems
>  Any complaints or grievances received from the public regarding ALPR camera placement or data use


I am not requesting personally identifiable information or license plate data. If any responsive record contains such information, please redact only that portion and produce the remainder. If any records are withheld, please provide a written index identifying each withheld record and the statutory exemption claimed.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jordan

title: Gabriel Garcia-Aviles ICE detention records

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Gabriel Garcia-Aviles ICE detention records
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. All electronic health records pertaining to Gabriel Garcia-Aviles, a 56-year-old Mexican national who died on Oct. 23, 2025, while in ICE custody at the Adelanto ICE Processing Center. This include medical encounter records, medical intake screenings, sick call requests, treatment notes, and any other medical or health-related documentation pertaining to Mr. Garcia-Aviles during his detention.

3. All Detainee Death Reports and mortality review reports related to Mr. Garcia-Aviles' death.

2. All administrative grievances filed by or on behalf of Mr. Garcia-Aviles during his detention, including any responses or disposition records.

Proof of death is not required, as Garcia-Aviles' death has been widely reported, including in an ICE press release: https://www.ice.gov/news/releases/illegal-alien-dies-victorville-medical-center-california-after-complications-alcohol

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Incident Summary: Officers located Allen, wanted on six felony warrants, hiding in a stolen van on Weyler Avenue. He was charged with four counts of attempted murder of a police officer and nearly two dozen other charges.
